---
title: "References"
nocite: |
  @*
---

livefigures stands on several decades of work in graph drawing,
declarative visualization, and reproducible publishing. The design owes
its central conviction — that figures should be *programs or notations
under version control*, not opaque binary artifacts — to the literate
programming tradition [@knuth1984literate] and its modern reproducible
research descendants [@peng2011reproducible], of which Quarto itself is a
direct heir.

The rendering stack draws on the systems below: Graphviz's layout
algorithms [@gansner2000graphviz] and the hierarchical drawing methods
they build on [@sugiyama1981methods]; the Vega and Vega-Lite grammars
[@satyanarayan2016reactive; @satyanarayan2017vegalite] descended from
Wilkinson's *Grammar of Graphics* [@wilkinson2005grammar];
force-directed placement [@fruchterman1991graph] for network layouts;
and the PIC lineage of embedded diagram languages [@kernighan1984pic]
that pikchr continues today.

## Bibliography

::: {#refs}
:::
